body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (Harita Nickel) stock price dropped IDR 20 to IDR 910 per share, resulting in a 25.6% decline in market capitalization to IDR 57.42 trillion, with a price-to-earning ratio of 10.15 and dividend yield of 2.94%. Compared to its initial public offering price of IDR 1,250 per share, the stock has fallen 25.6%, with controllers owning 84.35% of shares and a free float rate of 14.77%.
title: Harita Nickel's stock price plummets 25.6% from its IPO price, erasing IDR 20.87 trillion from its market capitalization.

body: PT Sawitmas Parenggan, a palm plantation company, has issued medium-term notes worth IDR 110 billion, consisting of three phases with principals of IDR 37 billion, IDR 37 billion, and IDR 36 billion, respectively, with a 5-year tenor and an 11.75% annual coupon rate. The notes, arranged by PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia and trustee PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Jawa Barat dan Banten, have been registered with the Indonesia Central Securities Depository and will have a trading transfer unit of IDR 1 billion.
title: PT Sawitmas Parenggan Issues Medium-Term Notes Worth IDR 110 Billion

body: PT Astra International Tbk plans to distribute an interim dividend of IDR 3.96 trillion, or IDR 98 per share, to its shareholders, with payment scheduled for October 31, 2024. The company's net profit as of June 30, 2024, stood at IDR 15.85 trillion, with retained earnings of IDR 187.38 trillion and total equity of IDR 254.18 trillion.
title: Astra International to Distribute IDR 3.96 Trillion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) weakened at the opening bell, falling to 7,526.71 at 9:05 am, down from 7,542.43 at 9 am yesterday, and remained in the red zone despite a slight climb. The decline affected other indices, including LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL, with several blue-chip stocks rising, while others, such as Unilever Indonesia and Garudafood, were corrected.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Falls at Opening Bell, Affecting Other Indices.

body: Prajogo Pangestu has increased its ownership stake in PT Barito Renewables Energi Tbk by purchasing 26,611,600 shares, demonstrating its support for the company's business expansion and development. This move is expected to contribute to Indonesia's energy transition agenda, as Barito Renewables' subsidiary, Star Energy Geothermal, plans to boost its power capacity by 102.6 MW through retrofitting and new capacity additions.
title: Prajogo Pangestu increases ownership stake in PT Barito Renewables Energi to support business expansion and development in Indonesia's renewable energy sector.

body: PT Sigma Energi Compressindo Tbk, an oil and gas service company, plans to distribute an interim dividend of IDR 3 per share, totaling IDR 2.73 billion, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on October 1, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 7.75 billion in the first half of 2024 and has retained earnings of IDR 20.99 billion, with a total equity of IDR 123.6 billion as of June 30, 2024.
title: PT Sigma Energi Compressindo to Distribute IDR 3 Per Share Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Avia Avian TBK has received dividends of IDR 107.99 billion from its subsidiaries, PT Tirtakencana Tatawarna and PT Tirtakencana Batamindo, with IDR 99.99 billion coming from Tirta Tatawarna and IDR 7.99 billion from Tirta Batamindo. The dividends originate from the retained earnings of the subsidiaries, recorded in fiscal year 2023, and do not have a material impact on the company's operational, legal, or financial condition.
title: PT Avia Avian Collects IDR 107.99 Billion in Dividends from Two Subsidiaries

body: Controlling shareholder PT Wijaya Cahaya Timber Tbk increased its ownership in the plywood industry issuer's shares to 60.8% from 57.34% through a recent acquisition, while other investors such as PT Arthakencana Rayatama, DBS Bank Ltd, and Low Tuck Kwong also made significant purchases. Conversely, investors like Antony Lesmana and PT Aladin Global Ventures released a combined 60.74 million shares in companies including PT Platinum Wahab Nusantara Tbk and PT Bank Aladin Syariah Tbk.
title: Multiple investors and companies in Indonesia engage in share transactions, with some increasing their stakes and others divesting their holdings.

body: Broom, a digital automotive startup, has concluded a Series A+ funding round worth $25 million, led by Openspace and other investors, to digitalize the traditional automotive sector through financing solutions for dealers. The startup has seen significant growth, with a 144.9% increase in fund allocation from its buyback product and $17 million in revenue from its leasing channel, which will serve as a foundation for its long-term success.
title: Broom, a digital automotive startup, raises $25 million in Series A+ funding to digitalize Indonesia's traditional automotive sector.

body: Seven bonds from different issuers will mature in October, with three issuers paying over IDR 1 trillion, including PT Sinar Mas Agro Resources Technology TBK's IDR 1.06 trillion bond and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK's IDR 1.34 trillion bond. The total payment value for these bonds reaches IDR 4.38 trillion, with other notable payments including IDR 250 billion from PT MNC Energy Investments TBK and IDR 200 billion from PT Summarecon Agung TBK.
title: Seven bonds from different issuers in Indonesia to mature in October with total payment value exceeding IDR 3.8 trillion.
